source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Cackling Blade
text: Those who carry the Cackling Blade relinquish all self control until the battle has long ended - often to their peril.

name: Ogre Blade
text: A broad and heavy blade, the Ogre Blade is, much like its namesake, an unsubtle weapon.

source_citation_book: Regiment of Renown: Prince Ulther's Dragon Company
name: Dragonblade
text: Armour Bane (1), Magical Attacks Notes: Dragonblade is inscribed with a Rune of Fury and a Grudge Rune, as described in Forces of Fantasy.

name: Sword of Battle
text: A simple weapon, yet finely crafted, blessed by mighty mages and revered priests.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Meteor Hammer
text: No matter who wields this mighty hammer, their blows will land with the force of a meteor plummeting from the heavens.

name: Duellist's Blades
text: Fine weapons, forged by the greatest of swordsmiths and imbued with potent runes of quickening.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Hell-forged Axe
text: In the midst of battle, smoke billows and flames burst from the glowing blade of this fell weapon.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Sword of Sorrow
text: Though it possesses no special properties when wielded in combat, it is said that a gesture with the Sword of Sorrow can pierce an enemy's heart from afar.

name: Dragon Slaying Sword
text: A mighty blade, wielded by many great heroes in their endless quests to slay foul monsters.

name: Headsman's Axe
text: A wide bladed axe, steeped in the blood of its countless helpless victims.

name: Spelleater Axe
text: Forged of rune-encrusted brass, the Spelleater Axe is anathema to the Winds of Magic.

name: Giant Blade
text: A broad and heavy blade wrought with powerful enchantments that only increase the weight of its blows, the Giant Blade is an unsubtle weapon.

name: Sword of Swiftness
text: Crafted from magically attuned materials and ensorcelled to strike as if with a life of its own.

name: Berserker Blade
text: Quenched in the blood of untold fallen enemies, the Berserker Blade eternally hungers for battle.

name: Sword of Might*
text: Bound around with powerful runes that increase its power and enhance the strength of its bearer.

name: Biting Blade
text: The serrated length of the Biting Blade cuts through armour of plate and mail with terrible ease.

name: Sword of Striking*
text: Engraved with runes of accuracy and swiftness, the Sword of Striking is an elegant weapon of great precision.

name: Burning Blade*
text: Once unsheathed, the cutting edge of the Burning Blade writhes endlessly with a living flame.

name: Armour of Destiny
text: The potent Armour of Destiny protects its wearer from all but the most grievous of harm.

name: Bedazzling Helm
text: A gleaming helm that confuses and confounds enemies, causing their blows to fall wide of their mark.

name: Armour of Silvered Steel
text: Polished to a mirror shine, the Armour of Silvered Steel encases its wearer from head to toe.

name: Glittering Scales
text: Each and every scale of this armoured surcoat is crafted from shards of precious metal.

name: Shield of the Warrior True
text: A heavy shield of oak and iron that turns aside all but the most deadly of missiles.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Levitating Shield
text: Born aloft by a cunning enchantment, this shield seems to place itself between its owner and the weapons of the enemy.

name: Spellshield*
text: The Winds of Magic lose their power when in the presence of the brass-bound Spellshield.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Trailblazer's Hauberk
text: The local flora seems to shift and bend to allow the wearer to pass unimpeded through even the thickest briar.

name: Armour of Meteoric Iron
text: This heavy armour was forged of ore mined from a meteor that fell from the heavens.

name: Enchanted Shield*
text: A finely crafted and sturdy shield that protects its bearer from harm time and time again.

name: Charmed Shield*
text: A simple shield hung with charms and fetishes to bring the bearer good fortune. In the heat of battle, such ornamentation rarely survives intact for long.

name: Dawnstone
text: The magic trapped within this semi-precious stone binds together shattered armour plates.

name: Talisman of Protection
text: A powerful magical aura surrounds this humble talisman, protecting its wearer from harm.

name: Paymaster's Coin*
text: This heavy golden coin may not be enchanted, but its weight in its owner's pocket certainly inspires heroism.

name: Obsidian Lodestone*
text: An air of chill hangs about obsidian, stilling the Winds of Magic in its presence.

name: Luckstone*
text: Can luck be trapped within a dull stone pendant? Possibly.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Icon of Fortitude
text: Carefully woven with protective enchantments, this exquisite brooch is said to shield its bearer from the deadliest blows.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Ironhide Talisman
text: It is said that the one who bears this powerful heirloom can endure even the most telling blows.

name: Banner of Iron Resolve
text: No regiment that stands beneath the Banner of Iron Resolve has ever fled from the foe.

name: Razor Standard
text: Hung with hundreds of wickedly sharp blades, an air of sharpness radiates outwards from the Razor Standard.

name: Rampaging Banner
text: As a matter of honour, those that fight beneath the Rampaging Banner are always first into the fray.

name: The Blazing Banner
text: An eternal flame flickers about the weapons of those that march beneath the Blazing Banner.

name: War Banner
text: A proud banner of great age. Those that march beneath the War Banner fight with grim resolve.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Banner of the Steadfast
text: Woven into the bright fabric of this banner is a scene of unimaginable gallantry, a scene that serves to inspire those who fight in its shadow to hold their ground no matter the odds.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Totem of Wrath
text: Cruel runes etched into the surface of this icon tremble with power, causing those around it to succumb to an animalistic ferocity.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Banner of Swirling Wind
text: Gale-force winds surge around this standard so fiercely that arrows and shot are blown forcefully away from their target.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Monster Hunter's Tapestry
text: Those who have earned the right to fight under this standard have battled some of the Old World's most dangerous beasts and lived to tell the tale.

name: Wizarding Hat
text: This splendid hat, once the property of a long dead Wizard, is still haunted by their ghost.

name: Flying Carpet
text: Woven from the finest silks in a far-off land, this splendid rug does more than warm the feet!

name: Healing Potion*
text: Purveyors of magical potions make many bold claims. Sometimes they are true, sometimes not.

name: Ruby Ring of Ruin
text: Uttering a simple command, the wearer of the ring can hurl a flaming ball of ruin at their foes.

name: Potion of Strength*
text: A concoction of strength-giving vegetables and roots, mixed together with a touch of magic.

name: Potion of Toughness*
text: Distilled by magical means, this potion is sure to make the drinker all but invulnerable.

name: Potion of Speed*
text: A powerful potion with a terrible aftertaste that sharpens the wits and speeds the reactions.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Arch-Lightning Rod
text: Crackling lightning fills the heavens with peril, grounding the creatures of the sky.

name: Potion of Foolhardiness*
text: A magical brew that fills the drinker with courage… it may actually just be strong liquor.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Potion of Fervour
text: This noxious brew allows those who risk drinking it a moment of godliness upon the battlefield.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Becalming Orb
text: This pulsing crystal orb can syphon the Winds of Magic away from its bearer's enemies.

name: Feedback Scroll
text: When read aloud, this charm causes ætheric energy to flood back towards the enemy Wizard.

name: Scroll of Transmogrification*
text: For a Wizard, there are few things better than the rush of joy felt upon turning a rival into a frog!

name: Wand of Jet
text: Carved from brittle jet, this wand refines a Wizard's power, but is easily broken.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Staff of Quietude
text: With a gesture, the bearer of the Staff of Quietude calms the Winds of Magic, causing spells and conjurations to dissipate into the other.

name: Lore Familiar
text: By creating a magical homunculus, a Wizard is able to better recall forgotten details of their studies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Scroll of Disruption
text: When spoken aloud, the words upon this ancient scroll cause the Winds of Magic to bluster and the spells of the enemy to unravel.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Tome of Spellcraft
text: Reading aloud from a dusty tome of great age, the Wizard enunciates their spells with unrivalled clarity and precision.

name: Power Scroll*
text: Unfurling a scroll bearing runes of power can greatly increase the spell's power.

name: Dispel Scroll*
text: The Wizard reads aloud a charm of unbinding from an enchanted scroll.

name: Arcane Familiar
text: Many Wizards are accompanied by familiars gifted with arcane knowledge. These creatures constantly whisper secrets to their master.

name: Earthing Rod
text: Inert metal runs the length of the Wizard's staff, drawing magical overloads safely into the earth.

name: Aspect of the Hound
text: Fighting as a pack, those in the kindred of the hound work together to bring down larger prey.

name: Aspect of the Bear
text: Elves are usually lithe and slight of build, but those who adopt the aspect of the bear are strong and muscular warriors.

name: Aspect of the Boar
text: The kindred of the boar are aggressive fighters who charge into the foe with reckless abandon.

name: Aspect of the Cat
text: Hunting from the shadows, the kindred of the cat are experts at hunting down prized game.

name: Master Rune of Adamant
text: The Master Rune of Adamant makes flesh harder than granite and more impervious than steel.

name: Master Rune of Gromril
text: In its purest form, Gromril holds runes better than any other substance in all the world.

name: Rune of Iron
text: Worked into suits of armour, the potent Rune of Iron increases the durability of the wearer.

name: Rune of Fortitude*
text: Runes of Fortitude turn flesh to iron and turn aside the enemy's blades with ease.

name: Rune of Preservation
text: The Rune of Preservation was first struck to counter the decapitating blows of Elven swordmasters.

name: Rune of Shielding*
text: In ages past, entire regiments would march to war bearing shields struck with the Runes of Shielding.

name: Rune of Stone*
text: A simple but brittle rune, the Rune of Stone is the first rune taught to apprentice runesmiths.

name: The Black Maul
text: This brutal, spiked cudgel is stained black with the blood of a thousand victims. Those that wield the club for too long become overwhelmed by its thirst for violence.

name: The Axes of Khorgor
text: When the Banebeast Khorgor was slain, his axes were taken by his successor and so they have continued to be passed down through the centuries.

name: The Brass Cleaver
text: The Brass Cleaver is said to be an artefact from another realm. Ancient and terrible, its outline blurs with a barely restrained thirst for carnage.

name: Scimitar of Skultar
text: The dark, serrated blade of this weapon is engraved with words of power in the Dark Tongue, keeping its biting edge keen and sharp for all time.

name: Full Plate Chaos Armour*
text: Chaos Armour is a gift of the gods, a symbol of the wearer's status as a true champion of Chaos.

name: Heavy Chaos Armour*
text: Chaos Armour is a gift of the gods, a symbol of the wearer's status as a true champion of Chaos.

name: The Fur of Sharggu
text: When the beast-spawn Sharggu was slain, its hide was roughly cut to make a heavy cloak.

name: Chalice of Dark Rain
text: It is said that to quaff from this blackened and rotted cup is to command the elements.

name: Cornucopia of Corpulence
text: This twisted basket overflows with such abundant filth that the weak might drown in its bounty.

name: Primeval Club
text: This ancient club, bound in cracked leather and infested with spiders and slugs, has been stained red with blood since the dawn of civilisation and is the bane of all order and hope.

name: The Gore Banner
text: Enemies tremble to see the foul sigils daubed upon this blood-drenched standard.

name: Axe of Men
text: A single, mighty stroke from this fearsome axe can cut even the most powerful warrior clean in half, a sight which emboldens the bearer's followers and horrifies their enemies in equal measure.

name: Banner of the Warped Moon
text: Glowing with Morrslieb's light, creatures of the sky cower before the Banner of the Warped Moon.

name: The Beast Banner
text: Adorned with shattered beast skulls, the Beast Banner radiates an unquenchable thirst for battle.

name: Mangelder
text: The Mangelder saps the strength of its victims' minds as well as of their bodies, and those few who survive wounds inflicted by the weapon rarely fight again.

name: Hunter's Spear
text: Crafted to fell the mighty beasts of the far north, this thick-hafted spear bears enchantments allowing it to penetrate yards of flesh and muscle, skewering the foe through-and-through.

name: The Soiled Tapestry
text: Dragged through filth, this once-sacred banner is now a symbol of hatred.

name: Horn of the Great Hunt
text: The dissonant blare of this twisted horn summons Beastmen to the blood grounds to hunt and slaughter the weak.

name: Pelt of the Dark Young
text: The Pelt of the Dark Young is shrouded in tendrils of darkness that gather about the wearer.

name: Scourge of the Burdened
text: Snatched from the petrified fingers of a cruel wagoner, the threat of this barbed whip inspires swiftness amongst all beasts.

name: The Blackened Plate
text: This battered suit of plate mail is blackened with the soot of burning palaces and temples.

name: Cacophonous Dirge
text: The march of the warherd is always accompanied by the raucous droning of pipes so deafeningly loud that it drowns out the instruments of all the foe.

name: Rune of the True Beast
text: This sigil of pure savagery is carved into the flesh of only the mightiest Beastlords, causing even the greatest of monsters to cower before the unbridled ferocity of this raging predator.

name: Blood of the Shadowgave*
text: When shattered upon the ground, clay jars of Morghur's blood boil and spit, birthing mewling beastlings that consume raw magic from the very air, before fading back into the cursed soil.

name: Dark Heart*
text: Beastmen rip the still beating hearts from their defeated rivals. In battle, they will feed upon these grisly trophies, claiming the strength of their vanquished enemies.

name: Totem of Rust
text: Little more than a jagged spike upon which the corroded remains of armoured warriors are impaled, the Totem of Rust spreads an all-consuming aura of entropy.

name: Daemon Heart
text: Pulled still beating from the chest of a shadow-skinned Daemon, this gruesome artefact must constantly consume magic lest it fade to dust.

name: Manbane Standard
text: The Manbane Standard is a corpse beset by carrion birds. Any who behold it are filled with the dread that it will be upon their eyes that the birds next feast.

name: Goretooth*
text: In bloody rituals, Bray-Shamans tear the teeth from the jaws of raging beasts to make amulets that allow them to tap into the primal rage of their victims.

name: Vitriolic Totem
text: This gnarled and twisted bough is hung with the heads of the fallen. From these rotted trophies drips a constant rain of blackened ichor and poisoned blood.

name: Banner of Outrage
text: This stolen standard has been befouled and stained as a terrible affront to the enemy. Its presence drives Beastmen to ever more vile extremes of savagery.

name: Stone of Spite
text: Contained within this glowing stone is the soul of an Ungor, slain in a hideous ritual. When crushed underhoof, a wave of bitterness and spite is released, banishing the Winds of Magic.

name: Horn of the First Beast
text: This immeasurably ancient, fossilised horn is said to be taken from the skull of the First Beast. When it is sounded, Beastmen find the savage fury of their race swelling within their hearts.

name: Skin of Man
text: The wearer of this flayed and sigil-encrusted human pelt is under a glamour, making them appear no more than a particularly ugly peasant. When the skin is cast aside, the awful truth is revealed.

name: The Plague Chalice
text: This rotted wooden chalice is filled with a vile brew of blood and pus. Any Shaman that sups from it is wracked with pain, even as raw magic courses through their veins.

name: Hagtree Fetish
text: A crudely carved effigy of a hagtree, this shamanic heirloom can spell doom for the Bray-Shaman's foe when brandished with the appropriate curse.

name: Jagged Dagger
text: This potent sacrificial tool radiates the pain and anger of a thousand ritual offerings made to the Ruinous Powers.

name: Mawseeker
text: Those tough enough to survive the pilgrimage to the Great Maw often return with part of themselves eaten away.

name: Mountaineater
text: Those able to scale the tallest peaks ritually consume part of the mountain to mark their conquest.

name: Giantbreaker
text: Any Ogre that has defeated a Giant in hand-to-hand combat can be, justifiably, supremely confident in their abilities.

name: Kineater
text: Having achieved their rank by killing and eating a member of their own family in a pit fight, Kineaters are considered ruthless even by their own tribe.

name: Longstrider
text: A Longstrider has hunted on the slopes of the mountains for decades, and is even capable of running down a sprinting ice elk.

name: Beastkiller
text: As a sign of their prowess as a hunter of monsters, a Beastkiller will adorn their body with trophy horns and tusks.

name: Deathcheater
text: An Ogre that has escaped certain doom is considered blessed by the Great Maw. Not surprisingly, Deathcheaters tend to have impressive scars.

name: Black Hammer of Hashut
text: This burning, black-hafted hammer bears the horned rune of Hashut, Father of Darkness, and has been carried into battle for centuries by the champions of Zharr-Naggrund.

name: Dark Maul
text: This mysterious weapon of unknown origin smoulders with a dark power that seems to eat the light around it and devours the warmth of its victims, leaving them withered husks.

name: Dagger of Malice
text: Said to have been found in an ancient barrow, this hooked blade whispers tales of murder and violence to any that grasp its hilt. Its edge has never dulled, no matter how many lives it has taken.

name: The Graven Sceptre
text: A badge of rank carried by the lords of Gorgoth, this iron staff carries the runic names of the masters of the Black Fortress since its founding, bound up with the baleful prayers of Hashut.

name: Armour of Bazherak the Cruel
text: Once worn by the Castellan of the Tower of Gorgoth, this armour was pulled from the Bitter Sea along with its owner's drowned corpse to stand as a warning to the arrogant of the price of hubris.

name: The Mask of the Furnace
text: This iron mask contains the essence and agonies of all who have perished before it in the fires of sacrifice.

name: Mantle of Stone*
text: Stitched from the flayed flesh of failed sorcerer-apprentices, a Stone Mantle preserves the wearer and reminds them of failure's price.

name: Hellshard
text: The malice of its wearer resides within the Hellshard's black depths, waiting to be unleashed on any who would dare spill their blood.

name: The Lammasu's Beard
text: This totem depicts the leering face of an old man, beneath which bristles a blood-stained beard torn from the chin of an ancient Lammasu.

name: Ashen Banner
text: Clouds of soot and ash billow from this smouldering pennant, concealing its bearers from their enemies.

name: Overseer's Sigil
text: Beneath this hateful banner the workers of Zharr-Naggrund must swear devotion to their dark masters.

name: Shroud of the Ancestor
text: Those that march beneath this black pennant, supposedly torn from the corpse of an unnamed Ancestor in ages past, seethe with rage towards their hated western kin.

name: Daemonic Familiar
text: The Chaos Dwarfs bind many daemonic creatures to their will. In battle, these entities cluster about their master's enemies, feeding hungrily upon the wards that protect them.

name: Breath of Hashut
text: Through the blackest of sorceries, the breath of Hashut can be sealed within an earthen jar and unleashed upon his foes.

name: Obsidian Vambraces
text: Carved of cold, hard obsidian, these powerful yet fragile artefacts ward against magic.

name: Daemon Flask
text: This rune-inscribed vessel holds a raging daemonic entity of phenomenal power. When unleashed its screams can shatter stone and cause the ground to split asunder as it flees into the æther.

name: Vial of Lammasu Blood
text: The blood of a Lammasu is a powerful ward against magic. Consuming it still warm allows a sorcerer to dispel the petty conjurations of their rivals with ease.

name: Scroll of Binding*
text: Chaos Dwarf Sorcerers make many foul bargains with Daemons, hoping to increase their powers. These infernal contracts are etched in blood upon scrolls of binding.

name: Slug-skin
text: This grotesque gift of the Chaos gods causes the recipient's skin to constantly ooze with a vile, foul smelling slime.

name: Crown of Horns (Beastmen)
text: A Beastman that sprouts an excessively sharp, large or twisted set of horns is supremely confident in their position at the pinnacle of the Warherd, so obvious are the Dark Gods' blessings.

name: Muscular Monstrosity
text: The Beastman has been blessed by the Gods of Chaos, and its torso bulges with huge muscles.

name: Pelt of Midnight
text: The Beastman's pelt exudes clouds of smokey darkness, hiding them from the eyes of the enemy.

name: Gouge-tusks
text: This Beastman's lower incisors have grown into sharp or excessively large tusks with which they gouge savage wounds into the flesh of their enemies.

name: Rune of the Beast Ascendant
text: The Beastman's horns have twisted into the semblance of a potent daemonic rune. This promise of ascendency to the ranks of Daemonhood fills its followers with courage.

name: Many-limbed Fiend
text: Some Beastman grow additional arms, allowing them to unleash even more carnage upon the battlefield.

name: Gnarled Hide
text: The Beastman's skin has encysted, forming a dense layer of crusty natural armour as tough as chainmail.

name: Uncanny Senses
text: The Dark Gods gift some Beastman with acute senses, additional eyes or even extra heads, granting them unnaturally fast reactions.

name: Æther Blade
text: The Daemon's weapon flickers in and out of reality, slipping through armour and into the soft flesh beneath.

name: Many Arms*
text: The Daemon has been blessed with a surfeit of grasping and clawing appendages.

name: Winged Horror
text: Gigantic wings sprout from the Daemon's back, allowing it to dive upon the unprepared foe.

name: Daemonic Robes
text: The Daemon is clad in shimmering robes that deflect the fury of incoming blows.

name: Banner of Unholy Victory
text: Scenes from a million battles fill the minds of those that gaze upon this banner.

name: Totem of Eternal War
text: The power of the æther flows from this accursed icon and into the Daemons that march beneath it.

name: Standard of Chaotic Glory
text: This banner flickers in and out of existence, creating a tear in reality through which the power of Chaos flows.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Unnatural Fortitude
text: Blessed by the Ruinous Powers with great resilience, some warriors' flesh can turn aside steel.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Dark Hearts
text: Those whose souls have been corrupted by the touch of the Ruinous Powers are chilling to behold.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Longstriders
text: Covering the ground with an unnatural haste, Longstriders are blessed with a daemon-like speed.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Enhanced Reflexes
text: Gifted with unnatural quickness, some warriors are able to slip the most lethal of blows past the defences of even the most skilled enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Battle Hunger
text: Possessed of an unwholesome thirst for battle, whole regiments race eagerly into the fray.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Brazen Will
text: There are those amongst the hordes of Chaos whose warped minds seem to resist the hostile magic of enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Prophetic Foresight
text: Granted brief glimpses of the future, a warrior may predict the movements of enemy scouts and foresee the arrival of reinforcements.

name: Executioner's Axe
text: A single blow from this huge, black-bladed weapon can cut any opponent in half.

name: Sword of Ruin
text: The wickedly sharp edge of the Sword of Ruin can cleave through armour as if it were air.

name: Lifetaker
text: Finely fashioned from blackest steel, Lifetaker fires bolts dipped in the venom of a Black Dragon.

name: Whip of Agony
text: An heirloom of the feared Beastlords of clan Rakarth, the Whip of Agony inflicts enduring torment upon its victims.

name: Shield of Ghrond
text: The Daemon-faced Shield of Ghrond consumes the strength of the enemy's attacks

name: Blood Armour
text: When anointed with the blood of the foe, this armour becomes ever more endurable.

name: Pendant of Khaeleth
text: The protection offered by this amulet grows to match the power of an enemy's attack.

name: Pearl of Infinite Bleakness
text: Swirled with red and black veins, this magical pearl emits a soul-numbing aura.

name: Banner of Nagarythe
text: The personal banner of the Dread-King proclaims his reign over the Elven Kingdoms.

name: Standard of Slaughter
text: Anointed with the blood of an Ulthuan Elf, this banner exudes a sense of bitter determination.

name: Banner of Har Ganeth
text: Under the influence of this ancient banner, warriors strike with deadly precision.

name: Cold-Blooded Banner
text: Those that march beneath this Cold One blood-soaked banner display a steely discipline.

name: Black Dragon Egg
text: When a Black Dragon egg is eaten, the properties of the Dragon are temporarily passed on.

name: Hydra's Tooth
text: Fangs are taken from the maw of a slain Hydra and crafted into deadly throwing weapons.

name: The Guiding Eye
text: Set in black iron, this oval ruby grants mystical sight to the wearer.

name: Black Staff
text: A Black Staff is the talisman of the High Mistresses of the Convent of Sorceresses.

name: Focus Familiar*
text: The Wizard channels their spells through the eyes of an arcane homunculus.

name: Tome of Furion
text: Furion of Clar Karond inscribed his knowledge onto sheets of flayed skin.

name: Higher State of Mind
text: So deep are the thoughts of the Mage-Priest that its physical body becomes a ghostly image as it slips further away from this reality.

name: Becalming Cogitation
text: With a belching croak, the Slann smothers the power of enemy spells.

name: Wandering Deliberations
text: The wandering mind of the Slann reflects upon the nature of many and varied magical disciplines.

name: Transcendent Healing
text: By force of will alone, the Slann heals the wounds inflicted upon its body.

name: Sorcerous Void
text: Closing its eyes and breathing deeply, the Slann Mage-Priest calms the Winds of Magic, causing enemy spells to falter and fail.

name: Harrowing Scrutiny
text: The unblinking gaze of this Slann Mage-Priest carries a measure of the scrutiny of the Old Ones.

name: Soul of Stone
text: Both the mind and body of the Slann Mage-Priest is hardened against the ill effects of magic.

name: Loremaster
text: Amongst the seers and scholars that reside within the Tower of Hoeth, there are those that practise both the mystical and martial arts as one. Such dedication marks a Loremaster from a mere scholar.

name: Shadow Stalker
text: Like all denizens of Nagarythe, Shadow Stalkers are outcasts. Many choose to travel far beyond the borders of Ulthuan, fighting unseen wars in the eternal defence of their homeland.

name: Anointed of Asuryan
text: In matters both martial and spiritual, the Phoenix Guard take their lead from the Anointed of Asuryan, learned sages that have for centuries been immersed in Asuryan's teachings.

name: Blood of Caledor
text: Ulthuan was once dominated by the valour and wisdom of Caledor's Dragon Princes. Today, the royal bloodline has faded, yet the haughty pride of Caledor's warrior elite still shines bright.

name: Chracian Hunter
text: In Chrace, a warrior's skill at arms and strength of character matters almost as much as the nobility of their blood, and the foremost warriors quickly rise to prominence within the ranks of the White Lions.

name: Warden of Saphery
text: When Ulthuan marches to war, the Swordmasters of Hoeth are led by the Wardens of Saphery, exemplars of the martial arts who have studied warfare and personal combat for decades.

name: Pure of Heart
text: Though Ulthuan's Golden Age is a distant memory, there are still a few nobles that embody all that is glorious about the High Elves, their presence reminding others of the heroic past of their race.

name: Sea Guard
text: Many nobles of Lothern choose to serve within the ranks of the Sea Guard, rising quickly to command the disciplined ranks of Ulthuan's navy and hone their skill on distant battlefields.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Maw Shard
text: Some have travelled to the broken lands around the Great Maw and brought back strange objects - among them the Maw Shard, an extra-worldly artefact.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Alchemist's Mask
text: Created in the House of Secrets of Shang Yang, this sorcerous mask gifts the wearer with the alchemical secrets of the Iron Dragon.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Dragon's Eye Banner
text: Under the scrutiny of the Celestial Dragon, enemy Wizards falter.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Icon of Heavenly Fury
text: The heavens are the realm of the Celestial Dragon, and those that would encroach upon his domain must face his wrath.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Standard of Wei-Jin
text: The banner of the Dragon Emperor inspires all Cathayan soldiers who gaze upon it, while spreading disorder and panic in their foes.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Learned Feng Shi Bo*
text: Some Wizards create Feng Shi Bo to aid them with their scholarly studies, tasking the spirits with remembering obscure lore.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Guardian Feng Shi Bo*
text: Feng Shi Bo are elemental familiars summoned by Cathayan Wizards. Most often, these earthbound spirits guard their masters, protecting them from harm.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Cloak of Po Mei
text: This enchanted cloak grants its wearer exceptional magical strength and mastery over one of the Elemental Winds.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Spirit Lantern*
text: The ghostly light of a Spirit Lantern reveals the denizens of the underworld and lays bare their weaknesses.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Banner of the Bastion
text: Those that would fight beneath this venerable banner must be as solid and unwavering as the Great Bastion itself.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Crown of Jade
text: Hostile magic recoils from the wearer of this gleaming crown of office.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Crystal of Kunlan
text: The crystals that grow in the peaks of Kunlan unleash sorcerous energy when struck.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Shield of Nan-Gau
text: Carved from a section of the Great Bastion, the Shield of Nan-Gau is a massive stone and iron shield.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: The Armour of the Warbird
text: The wearer of the Armour of the Warbird takes to the skies to strike at their enemies from above.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Sun & Moon Blades
text: According to legend, the blade of the Sun brings enlightenment, whilst the blade of the Moon brings only death.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Spirit Longma Spear
text: Made from the horn of the Great Spirit Longma Tian Wu and able to pierce any armour, heroes of Cathay carry this spear into battle with pride.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Jade Blade of the Great Fleet
text: The Jade Blade is an ancient weapon fashioned from the fang of a Lumbrian sea monster. Imbued with the monster's regenerative powers, it continually heals its wielder.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: The Monkey King's Wisdom
text: A huge and ornate club, studded with jewels and wrapped with precious metals, the Monkey King's Wisdom is a huge and brutal weapon.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Jade Armour of Beichai
text: Crafted in secret in the city of Beichai, this jade armour glows from within with unwholesome power.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Ring of Jet
text: Carved from purest jet quarried from the banks of the Dragon River, the Ring of Jet is infused with the power of Shiyama, allowing its wielder to summon ancestral spirits.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Sword of Reason
text: This great blade is sometimes wielded by Yuan Bo when his duties require him to enforce the Celestial Dragon's will upon the unruly.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Clockwork Compass
text: This finely wrought clockwork mechanism emulates the properties of the Wu Xing compass in Wei-jin. When correctly used, it can temporarily still the Winds of Magic.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Banner of the Dragon's Wrath
text: Those that march to war beneath the Banner of the Dragon's Wrath descend upon the foe with the fury of Li Dao, the Fire Dragon.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Sigil of Smoke & Powder
text: Clouds of gunpowder smoke hang about the bearer of this blackened sigil, obscuring them from the enemy's sight.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Brazen Blade
text: Crafted in the forges of Nan-Gau, this unusual weapon carves deep wounds that no magic can heal.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Golden Lion
text: Jade Lions are revered in Cathay for their bravery, and golden amulets carved into their likeness inspire great courage.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Jade Banner
text: Carved from purest jade by Cathay's finest artificers, this ancient banner fills the heart of a Jade Warrior with pride.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Seal of Xing Po*
text: The bearer of a Seal of Xing Po has studied the elemental winds for decades, mastering the Winds of Yin or Yang.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Mantle of Heaven
text: Woven from silk harvested from the slopes of Kunlan, the wearer of these robes is filled with a measure of the Celestial Dragon's essence.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Vermilion Quills*
text: Great heroes of Grand Cathay are awarded tokens of favour in the form of a Vermilion warbird's tail feather, tokens which are said to bless the bearer with unrivalled fortune.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Wrath of Xen Yang
text: Supposedly once wielded by the Celestial Dragon himself, this long and elegant spear burns with Dragon-fire.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: Scrolls of Wei-jin
text: Those fortunate enough to study the Elemental Winds in the heart of the Celestial City learn many arcane secrets. Such scholars carry with them many scrolls, heavy with knowledge.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Fires of Nan-Gau*
text: Cunningly crafted by the artificers of Nan-Gau, these earthenware jars contain the fury of the city's forges, ready to be unleashed upon Cathays enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Shroud of Shiyama
text: The spectral presence of Shiyama, the Spirit Dragon, lingers about those that fight beneath this silken standard crafted in her memory.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Banner of Xen Wun
text: Enemies often falter before this ashen banner, touched by the spirits of Xen Wun.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Dawn of the Storm Dragon
name: The Sword of Nan-Gau
text: This shimmering blade strikes with a life of its own, seeking any weakness in the armour of those that serve the Ruinous Powers.

name: Imperial Banner
text: This fine silken standard is a proud reminder of a time when the Empire was unified as one.

name: Hammer of Righteousness
text: This mighty hammer not only crushes flesh and bone but shatters evil with each swing.

name: The White Cloak
text: Blessed by Ar-Ulric, the magics within this fine cloak protect the wearer from the hottest flame.

name: Runefang
text: At the dawn of the Empire, a dozen magical blades were forged for Sigmar's chieftains. Those that survive are treasured artefacts, potent symbols of the status and authority of their wielders.

name: Armour of Fortune
text: Engraved with runes of luck and providence, the Armour of Fortune turns aside fatal blows harmlessly.

name: Pearl Daggers
text: Forged from burnished steel that glitters in the sunlight, the wielder of these lightweight daggers strikes with deadly speed and precision.

name: Armour of Tarnus
text: This cunning armour was crafted to the specifications of the warrior-Wizard von Tarnus.

name: Griffon Standard
text: A unit flying the Griffon Standard resolutely stands its ground, and few foes can break them.

name: Jade Amulet*
text: Potent jade talismans are given in thanks for services performed on behalf of the Cult of Sigmar.

name: Mace of Helsturm
text: This mace was used in battle by the first Grand Theogonist, Johann Helsturm. It is said that Helsturm's faith in Sigmar was strong enough to shatter the walls of a castle.

name: Sword of Justice
text: The Sword of Justice is an ancient weapon studded with Dwarf runes of vengeance and retribution that bestow the power of unswerving accuracy and deadly retribution upon it.

name: The Gleaming Pennant
text: Shining with an inner radiance, the Gleaming Pennant offers hope to those that march beneath it.

name: Blade of Silvered Steel
text: Those who wield this blessed weapon will hunt the dead and the damned with unmatched zeal.

name: Von Trickschotte's Wondrous Arquebus
text: This huge weapon shoots large calibre rounds capable of shattering a Troll's skull with a single shot.

name: Dragon Bow
text: A relic from the ancient Elven colonies of the Old World, this wondrously crafted bow enables the wielder to outshoot the finest archers in the Empire.

name: Banner of Duty
text: This venerable banner steels the hearts of despairing warriors, renewing their will to fight on.

name: Laurels of Victory
text: Celebrated heroes of the Empire are decorated with golden laurels which magnify their fearsome reputation in the eyes of their enemies.

name: Shield of the Gorgon
text: This burnished shield harnesses the petrifying power of the monstrous gorgons that inhabit the deserts of Araby.

name: Twice-Blessed Armour
text: Enchanted with magic and blessed by righteous hands, this armour stands as an example of what a unified Empire can achieve.

name: The Silver Horn
text: The triumphant call of this elegant hunting horn lends speed to the hooves of even the weariest steed.

name: Witch Hunter's Ward*
text: The hunters of witches have long carried blessed trinkets to shield them from the evils they stalk.

name: Shroud of Iron
text: This strange device resembles a market trader's awning. When unfurled, the Shroud of Iron protects its bearer from harm falling from on high.

name: Slayer's Hourglass
text: As the sand tumbles through the hourglass, the decapitating strikes and snapping jaws of monstrous beasts become sluggish and slow.

name: Book of Ashur
text: Written by an Elven Mage, this comprehensive book of magic is a priceless text for those that practice the art of magic... unless this copy is a poorly proofread and badly edited reproduction, that is.

name: Banner of the Knights Panther
text: The honour of bearing the heirloom banner of the Knights Panther into battle is known to only a few courageous individuals.

name: Wizard's Familiar*
text: Many Wizards keep familiars, supernatural creatures that aid them in their arcane studies. Often, this aid is apparent in the ease with which the Wizard dispels the conjurations of their enemies.

name: Tapestry of Sigmar's Triumph
text: Depicting Sigmar's heroic victories, this standard inspires others to meet the enemies of the Empire with weapons in-hand.

name: Wizard's Staff*
text: Throughout the Old World, Wizards carry ensorcelled staffs through which they can channel and direct their powers.

name: Icon of Morr
text: The spine-chilling influence of the god of death can be felt most keenly around this macabre totem.

name: The Banner of the Free State of Nuln
text: The City-state of Nuin has no stake in the War of the Three Emperors, instead taking pride in the prosperity of their great city.

name: Squintsoffen's Marvellous Magnifier*
text: Popularised by its namesake, this pocket-sized spyglass allows the bearer to more easily target distant enemies on the battlefield.

name: Ring of Fortune
text: Though soldiers are encouraged not to be superstitious or carry lucky charms, even experienced commanders know not to tempt fate.

name: Ring of Taal
text: By evoking the name of Taal, the primordial god of the wilderness, the wearer shields themselves in nature's grasp.

name: Twin-Tailed Wand
text: Though its origin is unknown, this impractical wand allows its bearer to unleash a torrent of magical force.

name: Tome of Midnight
text: As Wizards are often left to pursue their own paths to power, many end up perusing texts unfit for mortal minds.

name: Crystal Ball
text: Wizards who serve at court often rely upon reading portents and divining the future in order to best advise their masters.

name: Master Rune of Immolation
text: Devised so that war machines never fall into enemy hands, this rune is invoked only rarely.

name: Master Rune of Disguise
text: This rune magically distorts the shape of the war machine, rendering it almost invisible.

name: Rune of Skewering
text: During the War of the Beard, the Elves paid dearly for their help in crafting this rune.

name: Rune of Forging*
text: This rune ensures the war machine is free from imperfections and deadly accurate.

name: Rune of Burning
text: Shots fired by a war machine with this rune burst into flames when they strike the target.

name: Rune of Reloading
text: War machines inscribed with this rune are almost sentient in their willingness to allow repairs.

name: Stalwart Rune
text: The crew of this war machine will fight with great boldness to defend their beloved engine of war.

name: Master Rune of Slaying
text: The power held within this master rune has sent the likes of Giants, Trolls and even Dragons running for cover.

name: Master Rune of Piercing
text: Projectiles shot by this weapon punch through armour and flesh without slowing, allowing the wielder to cut down swathes of enemies in a single shot.

name: Master Rune of Bursting Flame
text: On contact with the enemy, the shot from weapons inscribed with this powerful rune explodes in a shower of burning fragments.

name: Rune of Concussive Force
text: Emblazoned upon the ammunition itself, this rune disorientates the enemy with a flash of light and a thunderous burst of sound.

name: Rune of Accuracy
text: Favoured amongst older Engineers, this rune helps the bearer to ignore distractions and focus in on their target.

name: Rune of Rapid Fire
text: Thanks to some clever innovation and a specially crafted rune, this weapon can unleash a salvo of rounds in the time it takes similar weapons to fire a single shot.

name: Rune of Molten Steel
text: This rune empowers the projectile to burn with the heat of the forge as it impacts the enemy.

name: Enchanted Rune
text: Simple runic enchantments help otherwise mundane ammunition to punch through magical wards and otherworldly armour.

name: A Blight of Terrors
text: These malicious spites of gruesome appearance dwell in dark places. They delight in terrifying unsuspecting intruders and take great joy from the horror they instil into their prey.

name: A Muster of Malevolents
text: Aggressive defenders of Athel Loren, Malevolents dart through the air in the wake of Asrai arrows, descending upon their victims with needle-like talons and vicious fangs.

name: A Befuddlement of Mischiefs
text: Mischiefs appear as glowing orbs of light that dart around the heads of their enemies, befuddling the minds of mortals.

name: A Murder of Spites
text: Spites often hide within the cloaks of their hosts, awaiting the opportunity to attack any who draw too near.

name: A Lamentation of Despairs
text: These creatures are much feared within Athel Loren, for they only appear in times of woe. Outsiders who feel their gaze upon them experience a deep and unnerving sense of dread.

name: An Annoyance of Netlings
text: Netlings scurry over their host with feverish abandon. Any enemy that attempts to strike the host will find their weapon snagged in a web of magical filaments.

name: A Resplendence of Luminescents
text: As they bound from branch, to bow, to cloak, Luminescents leave behind a magical spoor. Should they alight upon a weapon of any kind, their presence bestows a minor enchantment.

name: Dark Majesty
text: An air of oppression radiates from the champion, reducing the will of the bravest to that of a craven child.

name: Daemon-flesh
text: The Champion's flesh crawls with ætheric energy, turning aside the blows of the enemy.

name: Extra Arm
text: Gifted with an extra arm, the Champion rains many blows upon their enemies.

name: Diabolic Splendour
text: The air around the Champion seethes with chaotic energy, obscuring them from the arrows of the enemy.

name: Enchanting Aura
text: An enchanting aura hangs about the Champion, ensnaring the mind and slowing the limbs of enemies that venture too close.

name: Aura of Pain
text: The Champion's damned form exudes an aura of pain which wracks their enemies with crippling agony.

name: Master of Mortals
text: The Champion is worshipped as a god by their mortal followers, who see in them the power of Chaos made manifest.

name: Acid Ichor
text: The Champion's blood is a boiling, acidic ichor. Those who would harm them find themselves splashed with hissing, burning liquid.

name: Poisonous Slime
text: The Champion's flesh is saturated with a toxic slime that seeps from their skin, poisoning anything it touches.

name: Spell Eater
text: Like a predator feasting upon the flesh of its prey, the Daemon hungrily consumes the magic of its enemies.

name: Armour of Khorne
text: Clad in rune-laden armour, the Daemon's hide is all but immune to the blows of mere mortals.

name: Axe of Khorne*
text: Screaming with the power of a bound Daemon, this unholy blade thirsts for the blood of mortals.

name: Might of Khorne
text: Blessed by its patron, the Daemon's massive limbs bulge with strength and power.

name: Collar of Khorne*
text: The dread sigils inscribed upon this brass necklace provide protection against the most powerful of magics.

name: Nurgling Infestation
text: Multitudinous Nurglings swarm about the Great Unclean One and nestle within the folds of its skin.

name: Sloppity Bilepiper
text: The infectious tunes of a Bilepiper fill their companions with joyous murrain.

name: Spoilpox Scrivener
text: A Spoilpox Scrivener scrutinises the work of Nurgle's Daemons as they spread and record their master's blessings.

name: Stream of Contagion*
text: Opening its gullet wide, the Daemon unleashes a stream of noxious bile upon its foes.

name: Trappings of Nurgle
text: The Daemon is bedecked in rusted armour and rotted robes.

name: Infernal Enrapturess
text: Weaving a discordant melody upon an accursed harp, the Daemon enraptures those with the mage-sight.

name: Allure of Slaanesh
text: The Daemon is possessed of a peculiarly captivating and disarming visage.

name: Siren Song
text: The Daemon sings an alluring song, drawing its enemies to their doom.

name: Soporific Musk*
text: The Daemon exudes a heady and cloying musk that dulls the wits of its enemies.

name: Enrapturing Gaze*
text: The beguiling gaze of this Daemon holds the attention of all foolish enough to meet it.

name: Staff of Change
text: The mere presence of this staff causes all natural things to cry out in pain as it reshapes the world according to the Daemon's whim.

name: Will of Tzeentch
text: The Daemon perceives the intricate pathways of fate and can tug upon the strands of destiny should it so desire.

name: Power Vortex
text: The Winds of Magic billow about the Daemon, but remain beyond the reach of its enemies.

name: Iridescent Corona*
text: Glittering multi-coloured fire encases the Daemon's form, confounding the aim of the enemy.

name: Twin Heads
text: Possessed of two personalities and two intellects, this two-headed Daemon is a master of the Winds of Magic.

name: Woodsman's Axe
text: Wrought from purest Ithilmar, in the strong hands of a Chracian champion, this axe can cleave the skull of even the foulest beast with a single stroke.

name: The Vortex Shard
text: Hewn from a shattered Waystone, the Vortex Shard has the power to becalm the Winds of Magic.

name: Amulet of the Tempest
text: About this delicate and unadorned silver amulet the Winds of Magic boil with a storm's fury, confounding the wits of enemy Wizards.

name: Banner of Resilience
text: Adorned with a thousand emerald Dragon scales, the power of this ancient banner infuses the Elves that fight beneath it.

name: Circlet of Atrazar
text: This gem-studded circlet radiates an aura that protects its wearer from harm.

name: Armour of Stars
text: Decorated with glittering gems set in deepest blue lacquer, the Armour of Stars is a wonder to behold.

name: Ring of Fury*
text: The Elves of Ulthuan are known for their skill at crafting magical rings, and black iron Rings of Fury are popular amongst Elven warriors.

name: Sacred Incense
text: This bundle of incense, blessed by the wardens of the Shrine of Asuryan, will burn for days, wreathing its bearer with the scented smoke of the temples.

name: Bow of the Seafarer
text: Crafted from a single piece of rare silverwood and gifted to lanor of the Sea Guard by Finubar himself, this mighty bow is rumoured to be capable of sinking ships!

name: The Golden Shield
text: A highly polished shield inlaid with swirling patterns, the Golden Shield dazzles the foe.

name: The Trickster's Pendant
text: Though the pendants worn by devotees of Loec the Trickster are simple, they are loaded with power.

name: The Banner of Lothern
text: The Banner of Lothern is borne aloft by those that exemplify the martial spirit of the Sea Guard.

name: Banner of Balance
text: The fabric of this banner is steeped in magical energies and interwoven with powerful charms.

name: Staff of Solidity
text: The Staff of Solidity absorbs and dissipates malign energy, protecting its wielder from harm.

name: Star Lance
text: Forged from the metal of a fallen star, this weapon's graceful lines belie the formidable power it grants its wielder.

name: Gem of Courage*
text: Enchanted by Celaer in the reign of Bel-Korhadris, these small gems are awarded by the Phoenix King to the boldest heroes of Ulthuan in recognition of their bravery.

name: Blade of Sea Gold
text: Forged in the crushing depths and honed by crashing waves, this gleaming blade strikes with the unstoppable power of the ocean itself.

name: Banner of Confidence
text: This silken standard fills the hearts of those that fight beneath it with confidence.

name: The White Sword
text: A legendary blade forged for Aren, one of the first Swordmasters of Hoeth. The White Sword stands as tall as an Elf, yet its wide blade is as light as a willow switch.

name: The Blade of Leaping Gold
text: To the valiant, the Blade of Leaping Gold lends supernatural speed and vigour; to the craven and the corrupt, it brings only swift death. A more finely balanced sword than this has never been made.

name: Reaver Bow
text: Some believe this wondrous bow is the same one that was forged for Prince Arathion of Ellyrion a thousand years ago.

name: Foe Bane
text: This strangely twisted Elven blade was taken from the body of a Chaos champion. Quite how they came to possess it is unknown, as is the name of the brave Elf that reclaimed it.

name: Armour of Caledor
text: Every piece of the Armour of Caledor has been taken from the recovered fragments of now-lost armour that was once worn by a mighty Caledorian hero.

name: Dragon Helm
text: Crafted into the likeness of a great wyrm, the Dragon Helm remembers the heat of its forging, protecting its wearer from the touch of flame.

name: The Loremaster's Cloak
text: Wrapped in this pale grey cloak, the wearer seems somehow insubstantial and almost illusory.

name: Opal Amulet*
text: Potent yet fragile, an Opal Amulet offers powerful protection, but can easily be broken.

name: Banner of Arcane Protection
text: The heavy fabric of this banner is interwoven with powerful protective charms.

name: Battle Banner
text: This intricate banner, festooned with gemstones, bears detailed scenes of Ulthuan's victories.

name: Lion Standard
text: The renowned courage of a Chracian Lion fills the hearts of those who serve under this banner.

name: Banner of Ellyrion
text: This depiction of Korhandir, the father of all horses, imbues a regiment with tireless energy.

name: Null Stone
text: Those with the Mage sight see the bearer of this smooth, black stone pendant as a dark void, an unsettling patch of nothingness in the realm of magic.

name: The Cloak of Beards
text: An aura of fear and anger hangs about this grisly trophy of the War of the Beard, filling allies and enemies alike with unease.

name: Seed of Rebirth*
text: A seed from the heart of Athel Loren, gifted to the High Elves by their distant kin, the bearer is imbued with life-giving magic.

name: Sigil of Asuryan*
text: The sigil is tattooed on the hand of the Mage using inks prepared in the sacred flame of Asuryan itself. After use it quickly fades to nothing.

name: Annulian Crystal
text: Dug from beneath the Annulii Mountains, this crystal has been shaped by the magical vortex that swathes Ulthuan, so that it draws magical energy towards it.

name: Silvery Wand
text: This slender wand is covered with many intricately carved lines of winding runes in an ancient script.

name: Skull Totem
text: The pole of this banner is thick-set with the captured skulls of Khorne's victims, each marked with a brass-etched rune of slaughter.

name: Great Standard of Sundering
text: Runes of warding crowd close about this banner, their power focussed to deny enemy Wizards access to the Winds of Magic.

name: Icon of Endless War
text: This brass standard constantly drips blood, the smell of which drives all of Khorne's followers mad with battle-lust.

name: Icon of Eternal Virulence
text: Each drop of blood spilt beneath this dank shroud becomes infected with every affliction known to Nurgle.

name: Standard of Seeping Decay
text: A pervasive aura of pestilence surrounds this moth-eaten banner, sapping the health of all around it.

name: Rotten Icon
text: Pus and slime drip constantly from the Rotten Icon, turning the dry earth into noisome mud.

name: Banner of Acquiescence
text: Beneath the shadow of this standard, enemies lower their weapons and bear their breasts to the blades of the foe.

name: Rapturous Standard
text: Gripped by a strange euphoria, enemy formations crumble into confusion in the presence of this standard.

name: Siren Standard
text: A Daemonic Siren is imprisoned in this banner, its seductive song lulling the senses of all who hear it.

name: Banner of Discord
text: The air around this warped banner shimmers with the explosive unbinding of enemy spells.

name: Banner of Change
text: So saturated is this banner with the power of change that it distorts and mutates everything around it.

name: Icon of Sorcery*
text: The runes upon this banner swirl and distort as they magnify the sorcerous might of the Horrors that caper beneath it.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: Scroll of Withering
text: When read aloud, the incantation contained within a Scroll of Withering slowly and painfully drains the life force from the reader's enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: Scroll of Summoning
text: The words inscribed upon a Scroll of Summoning enable the reader to reach into the underworld and call forth the souls of the dead.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: Scroll of Awakening
text: When the spirits of the dead are awakened by the words of this incantation, they cluster about the living like moths about a flame.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: Scroll of the Outcast Dead
text: Upon reading aloud from a Scroll of the Outcast Dead, the souls of the damned are dragged screaming from the coldness of the void to torment the living.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: Scroll of Urgency
text: Scrolls of Urgency contain a simple but effective incantation which, when read aloud, imbues the undead servants of the Mortuary Cult with morbid vitality.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: Scroll of Righteous Smiting
text: In response to the words upon a Scroll of Righteous Smiting, the warriors of ancient Nehekhara redouble their efforts to strike down their enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: The Might of Miragliano
text: Tilea is famous for its mercenary companies led by skilled commanders of great courage and repute.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: The Renegade Knight
text: High born and haughty, renegade knights are often disdainful of lesser warriors.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: The Noble Outlaw
text: The Border Princes are filled with ruthless outlaws and cutthroats that claim to be of noble birth.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The War of Settra's Fury
name: The Wandering Diestro
text: Many a skilled duellist of noble birth has been driven from their home due to intrigue and treachery.

name: Crusader's Lance
text: In the hands of young Bretonnian heroes throughout the kingdom's many errantry wars, this keen lance has struck down scores of Orcs, Goblins and Beastmen.

name: Sword of the Quest
text: This large hand-and-a-half sword has been passed down from knight to knight, aiding generations of heroes on their noble quests.

name: Sword of Heroes
text: This mighty blade has powerful enchantments against evil bound into it. Its long and bloody history has seen its wielders kill enough fantastical creatures to fill a menagerie many times over.

name: Frontier Axe
text: Enchanted to ensure it never lost its razor-sharp edge while felling lumber, this tool, turned weapon of war, can sever limbs and sunder armour in a single swing.

name: Heartwood Lance
text: This unbreakable lance is crafted from the heart of the Major Oak, the largest tree in Bretonnia, which was shattered by lightning the moment Gilles was struck down.

name: Sword of the Stout Hearted
text: Imbued with the courage of Gilles le Breton himself, this sword fills the wielder with the fortitude to face down any enemy.

name: Foebreaker
text: Even those tough enough to survive a pummeling from this mighty warhammer are left disoriented and staggered by its heavy blows, making them vulnerable to any further onslaught.

name: Morning Star of Fracasse
text: This mighty morning star has a hatred of magical trickery and unholy artefacts. It will entangle and rip a magic weapon from the hands of any foe.

name: Gilded Cuirass
text: Inscribed with the words of the Grail Vow, the blessings of the Lady shine upon the wearer.

name: Anointed Armour
text: Whilst the wearer's faith in the Lady of the Lake holds fast, so too shall the wards of protection placed upon this armour.

name: Gromril Great Helm
text: This ornate helm, a gift from the Dwarf King Grundhar, bears a potent rune of protection.

name: Ironspike Shield
text: Covered in large, sharpened spines, this deadly looking shield is as dangerous as any mace.

name: Mantle of the Damsel Elena
text: Legend has it that Elena continued fighting the Goblin hordes even as the venom of a thousand spider bites coursed through her veins.

name: Grail Pendant
text: The power of the Lady floods through her most devoted warriors, giving them strength to endure the most grievous wounds.

name: Lucky Heirloom*
text: Though it may appear as junk to others, some warriors dare not enter the fray without some charm they had about their person when they first escaped death.

name: Sirienne's Locket
text: Sirienne, as skilled at conventional arts as she was at the art of magic, crafted this exquisite charm into a likeness of the Lady.

name: Banner of the Lady's Grace
text: This finely crafted standard depicts the Lady's first meeting with Gilles le Breton, the first king of Bretonnia, inspiring courage and valour in all who fight alongside it.

name: Valorous Standard
text: Knights who ride under this banner sing lustily and cry in joy for the sheer love of battle.

name: Conqueror's Tapestry
text: The morning after a battle, the victorious knights awaken to find their exploits depicted within this enchanted tapestry.

name: Crusader's Tapestry
text: Those that fight beneath this silken banner are inspired to ever greater acts of heroism, hoping that maybe their own exploits will be woven into its rich history.

name: Banner of the Zealous Knight
text: Filled with religious vigour, those who ride into battle under this imposing standard are eager to close with the enemy as swiftly as possible.

name: Errantry Banner
text: This tattered banner has travelled the length and breadth of the Old World and beyond, instilling its bearers with fiery zeal.

name: Banner of Honourable Warfare
text: Though no knight would ever take to the field of battle with bow or sling, those who fight under the banner pursue those who do with extreme prejudice.

name: Banner of Châlons
text: Awestruck enemies are unable to tear their gaze away from this magnificent banner.

name: Falcon-horn of Fredemund
text: This ancient horn was used by the Grail Companion, Duke Fredemund of Aquitaine. When blown, it emits a piercing cry, and the skies become filled with flocks of hunting birds.

name: The Seal of Parravon
text: Those with the skill-at-arms to win the famed tourney of Parravon are masters of their chosen weapons and carry the Seal of Parravon as a token of their victory.

name: Antlers of the Great Hunt
text: The knight's helm is adorned with a splendid set of antlers, the quarry of a great hunt slain by the wearer. The antlers denote exceptional prowess as a horseman and hunter.

name: Crusader's Clarion
text: Upon hearing the sound of this gilded trumpet, the warhorses beneath the gallant knights of Bretonnia thunder into battle with renewed strength and vigour.

name: Gauntlet of the Duel*
text: When cast down, this enchanted gauntlet magnifies even the tiniest scrap of pride or conceit into a surge of righteous self-belief.

name: Wyrmbreath Vial
text: By consuming the deep red liquid contained within this vial, it is said that the drinker can summon forth the fury of the great Dragons that dwell in the mountain ranges surrounding the Border Princes.

name: Silver Mirror
text: This lethal trinket is at first sight merely a vanity mirror, but woe betide the magic user that underestimates its true abilities...

name: Heart of the Wilds
text: Gifted to Damsel Elliea long ago by the Wood Elves of Athel Loren, this thrumming, mossy stone flows with magical power, drawing upon the strength of nature to fuel its wielder's arcane efforts.

name: Diadem of Power
text: When accompanying their baron's knights into battle, damsels and prophetesses have donned this jewel-studded band to help them channel the more destructive power that they wield in order to smite the Lady's enemies.

name: Sacrament of the Lady*
text: These thin, translucent wafers have been blessed by the Lady herself and imbued with a fragment of her power.

name: Prayer Icon of Quenelles
text: When opened, this prayer icon displays a meticulously rendered image of the Lady that inspires unfettered devotion.

name: Flamestrike Wand
text: Crafted from the charred branch of an ancient oak that was almost totally consumed in a forest fire, this wand allows the bearer to summon forth that same devastating inferno.

name: Order of the Knights Panther
text: Dedicated to the eradication of Chaos and its corrupt followers, these gallant warriors are amongst the most powerful of the Knightly Orders.

name: Order of the White Wolf
text: The Knights of the White Wolf swing their infamous wolf hammers with primal savagery, shattering shields and skulls with each blow.

name: Order of the Blazing Sun
text: The Knights of the Blazing Sun are vigilant students of war who strive for perfection on the battlefield.

name: Order of the Knights of Morr
text: Clad in jet-black armour, these dour warriors strive to protect the living from the ever-present threat of the undead.

name: Order of the Fiery Heart
text: These fanatical knights work closely with the Cult of Sigmar, earning a reputation as the shield of the Sigmarite faithful.

name: Virtue of Knightly Temper
text: With a heart of stone and resolute temper Beren stood unbowed, each sweep of blade spilling the blood of many.

name: Virtue of Heroism
text: Known as the Slayer of Monsters, Gilles fought and killed many great beasts that stalked the Massif Orcal, mounting their heads on the walls of his fastness.

name: Virtue of Stoicism
text: Awe inspiring are the tales that tell of grim Lambard, standing alone against evil hordes to save his dukedom.

name: Virtue of the Penitent
text: A penitent hermit-knight, Duke Corduin set aside his vows of solitude when summoned to fight for his king.

name: Virtue of the Ideal
text: Unmatched was Landuin, his chivalry above all others. Alas, as great was his skill, so too was his arrogance.

name: Virtue of the Impetuous Knight
text: In the glory of the charge was Balduin most alive. Though others better controlled their ardour, he was ever in the forefront of battle.

name: Virtue of Audacity
text: A master of feint and riposte, Agilgar's skill at turning an enemy's skill against them was legendary.

name: Virtue of Purity
text: Chaste and untainted by base emotions, Duke Rademund was the Lady's most virtuous champion.

name: Virtue of Duty
text: Most loyal of Companions, brave Duke Thierulf of Lyonesse did lament most painfully when Gilles did fall...

name: Virtue of the Joust
text: Great was Duke Folgar of Artois' skill at the tilt. None could withstand the thrust of his lance.

name: Virtue of Confidence
text: Haughty, proud and skilful, Carleond of Couronne took never a backward step in the face of adversity.

name: Virtue of Noble Disdain
text: Fredemund held in pure contempt those that would strike at their enemy from afar with a coward's weapon.

name: Virtue of Discipline
text: Famed for his discipline, Marcus took to the field of battle undaunted by his enemies.

name: Virtue of Empathy
text: A champion of the people, Martrud fought shoulder to shoulder with the peasants he protected.

name: Blade of Revered Tzunki
text: Supposedly wielded by the legendary Tzunki, this exquisite weapon hums as it cuts through the air.

name: Scimitar of the Sun Resplendent
text: As light as a dagger, this curved blade invigorates its wielder with the power of the sun.

name: Staff of the Lost Sun
text: Made of an alien metal, the Staff of the Lost Sun projects beams of hot light from its tip, searing the flesh of those caught in the blast.

name: Piranha Blade
text: The blade of this weapon is inlaid with thousands of tiny barbed teeth that rip and tear through the flesh of the enemy.

name: Shield of the Mirror Pool
text: Hostile enemy magic is absorbed by this ancient shield, and reflected back upon the caster.

name: Hide of the Cold Ones
text: The hide of a Cold One exudes a toxic slime that dulls the wearer's mind.

name: Glyph Necklace
text: This ancient token of protection wards off attacks, causing enemies to fumble their blows.

name: Aura of Quetzl
text: Blessed by Quetzl, this string of beaded shells, feathers and bones exudes a powerful protective aura.

name: Sun Standard of Chotec
text: Beams of reflected sunlight blind all who look too long upon the Sun Standard of Chotec.

name: Skavenpelt Banner
text: The stench of this ragged Skaven hide fires the blood of those who venerate Sotek.

name: Totem of Prophecy
text: Those who look upon this totem witness the truth and learn of their insignificance in the scheme of the Old Ones.

name: Jaguar Standard
text: Those in the shadow of this jungle beast hunt down their enemies with relentless ferocity.

name: Cloak of Feathers
text: When the wearer of this finely woven cloak extends their arms, the feathers rise up like powerful wings, lifting them into the air.

name: Venom of the Firefly Frog
text: Extracted from the rare and deadly firefly frog, even a tiny amount of this poison can prove fatal.

name: Horned One*
text: Horned Ones are a very rare, highly intelligent and preternaturally swift sub-species of Cold One.

name: Cube of Darkness*
text: Through careful contemplation, this small black cube may be opened for a fraction of a second, drawing the Winds of Magic into its depths.

name: Itxi Grub*
text: The Wizard carries a gourd containing magically charged (and delicious) Itxi grubs.

name: Cupped Hands of the Old Ones
text: A sphere of glowing light extends around the bearer as the divine protection of the Old Ones deflects the uncontrolled maelstrom of the Winds of Magic.

name: Glamour Weave Kindred
text: Though the Glamour Weave Kindred is far smaller than the others, its members are all potent spellcasters who play an important role in ensuring balance is maintained within Athel Loren.

name: Eternal Kindred
text: Those who dwell within the Eternal Kindreds spend the cold winters guarding Athel Loren's most sacred glades and groves.

name: Wild Rider Kindred
text: Those in command of the Wild Rider Kindreds are truly embodiments of their liege lord, reckless and ferocious almost to a fault.

name: Scout Kindred
text: Sometimes known as wayfarers or sentinels, the Scout Kindreds guard the borders of Athel Loren and observe the movements of those who wander too close.

name: Thundermace
text: Chiselled from a foundation stone taken from the base of a skycastle and bound with meteoric iron, the weight of a collapsing castle explodes from the head of the Thundermace.

name: Tenderiser
text: The destructive power bound into the twin heads of this massive two-ended club is enough to shatter a boulder with one strike.

name: Skullplucker
text: This heavy, spiked metal gauntlet was once worn by Big Agi Skullplucker, who enjoyed nothing more than twisting his enemies' heads off with this nasty-looking, bloodstained glove.

name: Bloodcleaver
text: This revolting, gore-encrusted weapon possesses a Vampiric energy, channelling the life force from its victims into its wielder.

name: Gut Maw
text: The wearer of this polished gut-plate is reinvigorated by the life force of their defeated enemies.

name: Mastodon Armour
text: This massive suit of armour was forged by the Chaos Dwarfs in exchange for many captives.

name: Spangleshard
text: This dirt-encrusted and tarnished bauble barely resembles the fine Elven ornament it once was, but its protective powers still linger.

name: Cathayan Jet Pendant*
text: Finely crafted Cathayan jet pendants are believed to protect against all forms of hostile magic.

name: Dragonhide Banner
text: This tattered trophy of a vanquished dragon passes its former owner's legendary ferocity onto its bearers.

name: Rune Maw
text: Hung with stolen Dwarfen talismans, the Rune Maw roars its anger when it detects magic.

name: Cannibal Totem
text: The Cannibal Totem protects those who devour the strong in honour of the Great Maw.

name: Bull Standard
text: The Bull Standard lends the strength of a charging Rhinox to the Ogres that march beneath it.

name: Jade Lion
text: Jade Lions are revered in Cathay for their bravery, and amulets carved into their likeness inspire great courage.

name: Daemon-Slayer Scars*
text: Daemonic beasts are hunted in the Warpstone Desert and bled into lead jars. This acidic ichor is used as warpaint, giving the wearer a truly terrifying appearance.

name: Fistful of Laurels*
text: Symbols of leadership snatched from the corpses of enemy commanders impress Ogres only briefly.

name: Halfling Cookbook
text: The culinary secrets contained within the Halfling Cookbook are a source of great power.

name: Hellheart
text: Biting into the heart of a Chaos Spawn, the Ogre creates a vortex of magical anarchy.

name: Grut's Sickle
text: Swinging the sickle of Grut the Bloodthirsty, a Butcher fuels their magic with the blood of their kin.

name: Sparkly Wizard Finda
text: Disappointingly, this arcane gem has never helped find a single sparkly Wizard, though it does shine brightly when used to beat one to a bloody pulp!

name: Bigger, Choppier Axe
text: While Warbosses love having a big, choppy axe, there is nothing they love more than a bigger, choppier axe!

name: Dead 'Ard Armour
text: This battle-scarred armour is made up of thick armour plates capable of turning aside the most telling of blows.

name: Staff of Baduumm
text: Once carried by the famous Orc Shaman Baduumm, this charred wooden staff still contains large reserves of the raw Waaagh! power that evaporated its namesake.

name: Necklace of Blessed Teef
text: Plucked from the jaws of enemies and fashioned into crude jewellery, the teef that hang from this necklace have been blessed by Mork (or possibly Gork) to protect the wearer as they thunder into battle.

name: Battleaxe of the Last Big Waaagh!
text: Vague legend tells that this huge, beaten battleaxe once belonged to either Gork or Mork. How true this is, there is no way of knowing, but it certainly is a great tool for hitting things with!

name: Da Angry Ladz Flag
text: Those who fight under Da Angry Ladz Flag hurl themselves at the enemy with careless abandon.

name: Martog's Best Basha
text: Warboss Martog the Mauler was renowned for his vast collection of 'legitimately acquired' weapons. The most prized of which was a massive Dwarfen hammer, covered with intricately carved runes and (since Martog's acquisition of the weapon) plenty of Dwarfen blood.

name: Grisly Trophy Rack
text: Covered in the decaying scalps and flayed skulls of conquered enemies, this totem is a foul reminder of the fate that awaits those who fall to the boss' blade.

name: Spiteful Shield
text: Possessed by an angry spirit and adorned with an enchanted maw filled with razor-sharp teeth, this shield snaps and snarls at unwary opponents.

name: Da Spider Banner
text: Created by the Forest Goblins of the Rootgrubba tribe, this unsettling banner depicts their fearsome arachnid god, whom the tribe reveres alongside Gork and Mork.

name: Idol of Gork
text: Before heading into a scrap, Orc and Goblin Shamans will craft small idols of Gork, the most brutal (but cunning) of deities to aid them in crumpin' their enemies and disrupting their magic.

name: Porko's Pigstikka
text: Most of the many spears wielded by Porko and his famous Boar Boyz lie shattered across a thousand battlefields. But this particular spear is special, having never broken nor lost its perfect point.

name: Effigy of Mork
text: Be it a string of lucky escapes, arcane enchantment, or perhaps the divine intervention of the cunning (yet brutal) Mork, this small trinket seems to keep the wearer out of danger.

name: Da Akrit Axe
text: In battle, this weapon seems to writhe in the hands of its wielder, seeking out the enemy's most vulnerable spots.

name: Da Hag's Brew
text: Few are sure on exactly what goes into the foetid drink, but its effect is undeniable, imbuing the drinker with the ability to harness the dank magic of the Troll Hags.

name: Da Thinkin' Orc's 'At
text: With the overpowering need to bash skulls removed from their mind, the wearer might now think twice about charging headlong into an Empire artillery battery!

name: Da Banner of Da Nomadz
text: Though their Waaagh! takes them the length and breath of the known world, those who carry this banner seldom seem to tire and are constantly on the move.

name: Da Choppiest Choppa
text: This broad, heavy bladed choppa boasts a wickedly sharp cutting edge. What's more, no amount of relentless chopping through armour and hacking through bone ever seems to dull it.

name: Banner of the Wildz
text: The multitude of natural elements used in the construction of this ramshackle standard seem to possess some minor magical enchantment, allowing those who carry it to traverse through foetid swamps, dense forests and rocky slopes without pause.

name: Wollopa's One Hit Wunda
text: The rebellious Goblin known as Wollopa wielded a wondrous weapon. Yet what he is most remembered for is his turn of speed, being able to easily outpace even the most irate of Orcs.

name: Backstabber's Blade*
