text: Being bigger and meaner than most, Orcs are happy to fight anyone, anywhere. Goblins on the other hand, are far happier sticking the pointy end of their blades into their foe's back.

name: Trollhide Trousers*
text: Foul smelling and uncomfortable, Trollhide Trousers possess remarkable regenerative properties.

name: Armour of Mork
text: This old and battered armour offers powerful protection against magic.

name: Glowy Green Amulet
text: This unimposing green amulet glows ever brighter as it absorbs magic. Sooner or later it is going to explode!

name: The Collar of Zorga
text: Zorga was a respected War Boar wrangler. When his magical collar was stolen, he became a snack for his porcine pets.

name: The Big Red Raggedy Flag
text: This tattered banner belonged to the great hero, Rowdy Porker, whose spirit imbues it to this day.

name: Waaagh! Banner
text: Blessed by an Orc Shaman, this banner fills those that march under it with a great sense of urgency.

name: Da Banner of Butchery
text: This rough-hewn totem has absorbed a great amount of primal Orc fury.

name: Guff's Windy Banner
text: The banner of Guff's Garrison eternally flutters in an inexplicable breeze. Those that fight in its shadow display remarkable intestinal fortitude.

name: Big Boss 'At
text: Orcs and Goblins are easily impressed, and this ornate helmet is particularly impressive. What's more, the Boss insists that wearing it makes them braver and cleverer, which must be true.

name: 'Eadbuttin' 'At*
text: 'Eadbuttin' contests are popular in Orc culture. Especially cunning (or brutal) participants employ magical means to improve their odds.

name: Fungus Wine*
text: Night Goblins cultivate many strange fungi, the most potent of which are fermented into mind-altering wines.

name: Glittering Wotnots
text: Supposedly made from the shards of a shattered magic mirror, these gaudy gems hung from a Shaman's staff will sometimes reflect hostile magic back upon its caster.

name: Buzgob's Knobbly Staff
text: The famed Great Shaman Buzgob once attempted to channel so much Waaagh! magic that he vanished in a flash of light, leaving behind only his staff and sticky green mist.

name: Idol of Mork
text: On the eve of battle, Orc and Goblin Shamans often craft small idols of Mork, hoping that the most cunning (but brutal) of deities will watch over them.

name: Rune of the Dishonoured
text: This dreadful mark prolongs the shame of the one who bears it.

name: Rune of Endless Battle
text: Those who bear this tattoo struggle to contain their rage once battle begins.

name: Rune of the Reckless
text: Slayers that bear this tattoo fight with a burning and uncontrollable fury.

name: Rune of the Hateful
text: This unpleasant tattoo fills its bearer with hate and bitterness.

name: Rune of Grit
text: The flesh of those marked with this rune takes on the texture of stone.

name: Rune of the Dauntless
text: Those marked with this rune seek the deadliest foes to challenge in single combat.

name: Rune of Wrath
text: Those that bear this rune upon their flesh are filled with wrath.

name: Warrior's Rune
text: Only the most skilled of warriors are permitted to bear this mark upon their flesh.

name: Rune of Blazing Fury
text: The rage of the ancestors burns brightly within this fiery rune.

name: The Fellblade
text: This most terrible of weapons was forged to slay Nagash himself. No foe can stand before it and even the wielder must eventually succumb to its baleful effects.

name: Weeping Blade
text: This weapon weeps a venom so corrosive it can melt through armour with ease to deeply pierce and poison the flesh beneath.

name: Blade of Nurglitch
text: Blessed by the Plaguelord of Clan Pestilens, even a scratch from this filth-encrusted weapon can turn into instant infection.

name: Death Globe*
text: Warlock Engineers often carry much improved and far more deadly versions of the globes wielded by Poisoned Wind Globadiers.

name: Warpstone Armour*
text: Cunningly wrought of warpstone-infused iron, this heavy suit of armour protects without stifling the Winds of Magic.

name: Cautious Shield
text: This large, magical shield emphasises an already prominent trait of Skaven nature.

name: Shadow Magnet
text: Warpstone-contaminated veins of ore can absorb light and cast an aura of deep, shadowy darkness.

name: Warpstone Amulet
text: The Warpstone Amulet radiates disharmony and confusion, so that anyone who attacks its bearer becomes dazed and bewildered.

name: Storm Banner
text: This ancient banner possesses the power to wrack the sky with storms and tear the heavens apart with its fury.

name: Grand Banner of Superiority
text: This ragged shroud, emblazoned with clan signs, proclaims the superiority of the Warlord Clans.

name: Dwarf Hide Banner
text: Made from the tanned hides and shorn beards of vanquished Dwarfs, this banner fills the Skaven with the courage to face their ancient enemies.

name: Banner of Verminous Scurrying
text: A great, skittering urgency fills those that scurry beneath this tattered banner.

name: Brass Orb
text: This fist-sized orb of whirling cogs is capable of opening a crack into the twisting Realm of Chaos.

name: Skalm
text: This rare healing balm is smeared on wounds and is even rumoured to prolong a Skaven's life.

name: Skavenbrew*
text: Brewed from warpstone and blood, Skavenbrew drives humble Skaven into a fighting frenzy.

name: Warp Condenser
text: This rune-inscribed power pack is covered with copper coil wiring, flywheels, and enchanted mechanisms to draw extra energy from the fickle Winds of Magic.

name: Storm Daemon
text: Created by the Warlock Engineers of Clan Skryre, this warpstone-powered device crackles with barely contained power.

name: Warpstone Tokens*
text: Pieces of refined warpstone are consumed by power-hungry Skaven Wizards.

name: Master Rune of Grungni
text: This rune stirs the Winds of Magic until their swirling forces protect those that march beneath it.

name: Master Rune of Stromni Redbeard
text: This rune was first carved onto the Battle Standard of Durgin, son of Grindo, son of Grimnir.

name: Master Rune of Hesitation
text: The presence of this rune causes the enemy to hesitate before the Dwarfen throng and falter in their charge.

name: Rune of Confusion
text: Under the shadow of this rune, the enthusiasm of charging enemies quickly wanes.

name: Rune of Fear
text: Those that carry this banner to battle seem to tower over the enemy, looming like mythical giants.

name: Rune of Battle
text: In ages past, even lesser strongholds and fortified mines had a standard bearing this humble rune.

name: Strollaz' Rune
text: Tireless by nature, Dwarfs that march beneath Strollaz' Rune become relentless in their advance.

name: Rune of Courage
text: This inspirational rune further bolsters the already considerable courage of all Dwarfs near it.

name: Master Rune of Calm
text: When the power of this rune is unleashed, the Winds of Magic are stilled, leaving enemy Wizards unable to cast even the most simple of spells.

name: Master Rune of Balance
text: Forged in the glowing embers of a burning book of spells, this rune hungers after the Winds of Magic.

name: Master Rune of Spite
text: Created to protect the gates of Dwarfen holds, this rune is cunningly used on many other devices.

name: Rune of Spellbreaking*
text: It is said that this powerful rune was first crafted by Grungni and Valaya working as one.

name: Rune of Warding*
text: Runes of Warding are potent but fragile, and their magic easily expended all too soon.

name: Rune of Luck*
text: The runesmith who first struck this rune supposedly acquired a fortune in gold through gambling.

name: Rune of the Furnace
text: Intended to aid Dwarfs working hot forges, this rune has been adapted for use in battle.

name: Rune of Passage
text: This rune causes even the most treacherous of terrain to part and become level underfoot.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Blade of Antarhak
text: Those cut by the Blade of Antarhak age by decades in the blink of an eye, their vitality stolen and bestowed upon the wielder of this fell blade.

name: Destroyer of Eternities
text: Imbued with powerful incantations, the great blade of this scything weapon is impossibly sharp and is said to destroy the souls of its victims, denying them any hope of reaching the afterlife.

name: Crook & Flail of Radiance
text: These gleaming golden weapons represent the high status of the bearer, and all that enter their presence are humbled by the aura of majesty these weapons convey.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: The Conqueror's Blade
text: Many a king and queen, warlord and emperor foolish enough to resist the rule of the Lord of Nehekhara have found their heads parted from their bodies by this wicked weapon.

name: Flail of Skulls
text: This flail is made from the gilded skulls of conquered enemies. Foes struck by these skulls are lacerated and savaged by their sharpened teeth, and bleed profusely from their wounds.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Phakth's Blades of Justice
text: Crackling lightning arcs from these twin blades as they slice through the ranks of Nehekhara's foes.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Staff of Aeons
text: A single blow from this accursed weapon can cause armour to rust and tarnish in a matter of moments, leaving those wearing it vulnerable to the Liche Priest's minions.

name: Serpent Staff
text: Crafted in the likeness of a rearing cobra, this tall, heavy-headed staff writhes with an unnatural life, striking at the enemies of its bearer with venomous fangs.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Royal Mantle
text: It is said that the wisdom of the kings and queens of Nehekhara is held within these armoured plates, giving counsel to the wearer as they command their warriors on the battlefield.

name: Armour of the Ages
text: Forged from bronze, cooled in blood and plated with gold, this ancient armour can turn aside even the strongest blow.

name: Shield of Ptra
text: Bursting from the face of this ornate shield, the radiance of the Sun God Ptra blinds the enemy.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Warding Splint
text: Protective strips of bronze and iron inscribed with regenerative hieroglyphics are placed across the Liche Priest's shrivelled body, for without their power the armies of the Tomb Kings will crumble.

name: Crown of Kings*
text: Ancient and ornate crowns are a symbol that servitude extends beyond the bounds of death.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Amulet of the Serpent
text: Cast in antique gold with glittering emeralds for eyes, this magical snake amulet laces the weapons of nearby Nehekharan warriors with a fast-acting and deadly venom.

name: Collar of Shapesh
text: This ornate collar is a powerful charm that trades the life of one for that of another.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Relic of the Desert Sun
text: With their withered, mummified forms vulnerable to open flame, some Tomb Kings placed their faith in the sun god Ptra to protect them.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Royal Standard of Settra
text: When Settra's personal banner marches to war, it is an omen of doom for those who stand against him. For the True King of Nehekhara kneels to no one; all shall bow to him or face annihilation.

name: Standard of the Cursing Word
text: This potent icon contains a powerful curse, similar to that which protects the mortal remains of the ancient monarchs of Nehekhara.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Sigil of Centuries
text: Time behaves strangely around this odd icon, the weight of years slowing even the most agile warriors to a sluggish crawl.

name: Icon of the Sacred Eye
text: The arcane power contained within this unblinking eye guides the arms of those that march beneath it.

name: Icon of Rakaph
text: Those that march beneath the banner of King Rakaph II are instilled with unrivalled discipline.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Tapestry of Conquered Lands
text: Woven from the standards of vanquished enemies, this patchwork banner serves as a warning to those who stand against the rulers of Nehekhara.

name: Mirage Banner
text: Shimmering with the heat of the desert, this banner shifts constantly, confusing the eye of the enemy.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Banner of the Desert Winds
text: This tattered banner snaps and waves under the command of a spectral breeze, whose gusts drive forward those who march under it.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Staff of Awakening
text: Carried only by the most revered Liche Priests, this powerful artefact amplifies the wielder's already formidable necromantic talents.

name: Cloak of the Dunes
text: This tattered cloak is infused with the magic of the deserts, enabling its wearer to transform into an abrasive storm of sand that cuts through the foe.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Orb of Ptra
text: Upon uttering a prayer to the mighty Ptra, this orb becomes as bright as the midday sun, confounding enemy archers.

name: Icon of Rulership*
text: The hero's chariot bears a totemic standard that pulses with greatness and proclaims their many victories over their enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Phâzerakt's Kanopi
text: It is said that Phâzerakt's spirit can be unleashed from within the ornate vessel in which it is trapped to aid in a mass summoning.

name: Death Mask of Kharnutt
text: The expressionless Death Mask of Kharnutt has borne witness to the demise of untold thousands over its long millennia, causing an aura of dread and despair to hang heavily about it.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Curse-Weaver Wand
text: The power within this accursed wand seeks only to enfeeble the enemies of its wielder.

name: Enkhil's Kanopi
text: Within this dusty clay vessel resides the wicked heart of the Liche Priest Enkhil. Should it ever be opened, the spirit of the jealous priest consumes the magic of his rivals.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: Tablets of Tahoth*
text: These powerful relics of Tahoth's wisdom crumble to dust once their power is spent.

name: Hieratic Jar*
text: This earthenware jar, sealed with wax and bound with glyphs, contains the magically charged remains of a once-mighty Liche Priest.

name: Scarab Brooch
text: From far beneath the desert sands, scuttling dead things are drawn towards this blackened brooch.

name: Frostblade
text: This blade of blue ice-steel is bound with such deadly spells that its merest touch will suck the soul from its victim's body.

name: Sword of Kings
text: This accursed blade of ancient and ageless design thirsts constantly for the souls of its wielder's enemies.

name: Blood Drinker
text: This long, slender blade greedily drinks the blood of its victims, their life force revitalising its master.

name: Dreadlance
text: Once wielded by an infamous Vampire knight, this lance is uncannily accurate, said to find its target even in the dark of night.

name: The Flayed Hauberk
text: Stitched from the flesh of countless victims, the wearer of the Flayed Hauberk is enshrouded and protected by the souls of the damned.

name: The Accursed Armour
text: Some strange flaw renders the wearer of this armour resilient beyond measure, yet robs them of dexterity.

name: Von Carstein Ring
text: An ancient heirloom of the lords of Sylvania, this ring is known to make a Vampire carrying it almost impossible to kill.

name: Crown of the Damned
text: The wearer of the Crown of the Damned draws revitalising energies from the spirits imprisoned within it, but at times their eternal wailing can become overpowering.

name: Banner of the Barrows
text: Woven from the wind and the cold, the chill of this banner touches the hearts of those who stand before it.

name: Drakenhof Banner
text: The infamous Count Vlad von Carstein had thrall Necromancers enchant his household standard to sustain his bodyguard.

name: The Screaming Banner
text: Even the bravest warriors tremble in the presence of a haunted banner that wails a constant dirge.

name: Standard of Hellish Vigour
text: The restless essence of the Vampire whose flesh was flayed to craft this banner invigorates the Undead nearby.

name: Helm of Commandment
text: This ancient and corroded helmet can be used to infuse Undead servants with the wearer's sentience, making them formidable fighters.

name: Hand of Dust
text: The severed hand of the Great Necromancer himself, the mere proximity of this withered appendage can drain all life and vitality from mortals, leaving them as desiccated husks.

name: Cloak of Mist & Shadows
text: This cloak frees the wearer from the bonds of their physical form, allowing them to move through solid matter like a ghost.

name: Skull Staff
text: The Skull Staff constantly whispers to its bearer, revealing the secrets of their foes and predicting the ebb and flow of the Winds of Magic.

name: Sceptre of De Noirot
text: The Necromancer de Noirot suffered a terrible demise when he invoked the power of this sceptre and raised more of the Undead than he could control.

name: Spell Familiar*
text: A spell familiar memorises a spell on its master's behalf, constantly rehearsing for its big moment until it is called upon to share its arcane knowledge.

name: Curse of the Revenant
text: Through magic, bloodletting or sheer force of will, the strength of this Vampire to survive the centuries is beyond compare.

name: Beguile
text: The Vampire has an entrancing gaze that mesmerises their foes, rendering them all but incapable of causing the Vampire harm.

name: Flying Horror
text: The Vampire is able to take to the skies with great, membranous wings, or perhaps can even metamorphose into a monstrous bat.

name: Dark Acolyte
text: Some Vampires have an affinity for necromantic magic beyond that of their peers, which only increases with age and practice.

name: Master of the Black Arts
text: The Winds of Magic bend easily to the will of this Vampire, so matchless is their magical prowess.

name: Supernatural Horror
text: This Vampire is hideous to look upon, having long since forsaken the trappings of nobility and beauty and embraced the beast within.

name: Lord of the Night
text: The Vampire howls into the cold night, their cry answered by the baying of countless dark creatures.

name: Daemonsword
text: This most deadly of blades is a prison for the essence of a powerful but treacherous Daemon. For all the might it grants its wielder, the Daemon within endlessly strives to be set free.

name: Chaos Runesword
text: This evil blade was forged from black gromril by the thrice-cursed Dwarf Runesmith Grugni Ironheart, a secret pawn of the Chaos gods.

name: Filth Mace
text: This heavy-headed and rust-encrusted mace endlessly oozes a necrotic slime which can sap the strength and vitality of the strongest warriors, causing even minor wounds to become debilitating.

name: Spellthieving Sword
text: Forged of magically resistant ores and carved with dire runes of spell warding, the Spellthieving Sword burns and blinds its victims, robbing them of their mage sight.

name: Obsidian Dread-Glaive
text: This cruel weapon has been used to sever heads and limbs in the hands of the Dark Gods' most dangerous champions.

name: Armour of the Damned
text: This ornate suit of Chaos armour shimmers with the eldritch energies of the Chaos Wastes, distorting the wearer's outline and clouding the minds of their foes.

name: Crimson Armour of Dargan
text: The rich crimson metal of this armour flares into a storm of bloodcoloured light when its wearer wills it, dazzling those who would strike a mortal blow.

name: Dagger of the Dark Pantheon
text: By drawing on the lifeforce of those slain with this wicked weapon, its wielder is able to channel the Winds of Magic far more recklessly.

name: Chieftain's Blade
text: The title of Chieftain is earned and kept through triumph in single combat, not by birthright.

name: Daemonic Platemail
text: This infernal armour writhes with the leering and screeching faces of the daemonic spirits that infest it.

name: Crown of Everlasting Conquest
text: The warrior's helmet is crafted into a magnificent crown of spikes and horns which radiate invigorating dark power.

name: Taskmaster's Scourge
text: This devilish weapon is covered in barbed spines, whose bite drives the followers of Chaos into battle.

name: Brazen Collar*
text: Champions are sometimes gifted with heavy, rune-etched brass collars. Worn around the neck, these marks of favour are potent wards against magic.

name: Mighty Serpent's Scalemail
text: A dark enchantment wrought in the name of the Serpent has been placed upon the glittering scales of this armour.

name: Talisman of the Carrion Crow
text: The magical wards that venerate the Crow blesses the wearer with otherworldly resilience and empowers their strikes with deadly poisons.

name: Banner of the Gods
text: Forged in the red-lit depths of Zharr-Naggrund, the Banner of the Gods induces dread in the enemy and unshakeable courage in the servants of Chaos.

name: Doom Totem
text: A collection of ragged skins strung over a framework of bone, the Doom Totem exudes a potent magic that demoralises and appals all who look upon it.

name: Talisman of the Soaring Eagle
text: Those who wear the mark of the Eagle find the potency of their opponents' magic wane in their presence.

name: Blasted Standard
text: The Blasted Standard burns with coruscating magical flames that explode outward to consume any missile that comes near it.

name: Banner of Rage
text: Sewn from strings of congealed gore, this banner radiates a bloodlust so strong that those beneath it are goaded into a state of permanent rage.

name: Pendant of Damnation
text: This pendant burrows deep into the owner's chest and nestles beside their heart, filling them with vitality even as their mortal form inches towards damnation.

name: Banner of the Dark Powers
text: Pulsing with iridescent light, this banner shields those who march beneath it from all but the most potent magic.

name: Banner of the Baying Hound
text: Those who carry the banner of the Baying Hound race towards their foes, eager to shed the blood of their enemy.

name: Helm of Many Eyes
text: This ornate full face helm has no eyeholes, but is covered with magical eyes that, it is said, can see into the souls and minds of men.

name: Sea Raider's Crest
text: The flags flown by notorious raiders strike fear into the hearts of those who eke out an existence by the sea.

name: Favour of the Gods*
text: The Champion bears an obsidian pendant that marks them as having truly earned the favour of the Dark Gods.

name: Icon of Darkness
text: This accursed icon is cloaked in endless shadow, the darkness shielding those who bear it from view as they march.

name: Bloodskull Pendant
text: Filled with the blood of a Daemon Prince of Khorne, this accursed amulet turns the wearer into a maelstrom of violence.

name: Skull of Katam
text: The polished skull of the daemonologist Katam constantly whispers its dark secrets into the mind of any Wizard nearby.

name: Rod of the Damned
text: Forged in the depths of the Realm of Chaos, this infernal rod sunders those who resist the will of Chaos.

name: Infernal Puppet
text: Twisting and jerking upon its strings, this eldritch homunculus draws the Winds of Magic into itself, causing them to flow with strange currents that confound the wits of its master's rivals.

name: Daemon-Forged Barding
text: The wicked, hell-forged blades that cover this barding slice and maim the enemy as the rider crashes through their ranks.

name: Sceptre of Power
text: Those who have carried the Sceptre have enjoyed the thrill of limitless power, but each of them paid a heavy price for the privilege.

name: Grimoire of Ogvold
text: Ogvold feverishly documented many arcane secrets over the years, hoping that this knowledge would serve the will of Chaos even after his demise.

name: Tome of the Dark Gods
text: This ancient text is filled with the maddening scrawlings and forbidden knowledge of both daemonic and mortal sorcerers alike.

name: Master Rune of Smiting
text: The secret of this rune was preserved in the Rundrokikron, an ancient tome kept in the treasure troves of Karak Kadrin.

name: Master Rune of Skalf Blackhammer
text: Runesmith Skalf forged many hammers of great might, and some maintain that even the hammer of Sigmar, Ghal Maraz itself, was his work.

name: Master Rune of Alaric The Mad
text: Alaric the Mad forged the famous Runefangs for the Elector Counts of the Empire before meeting an unknown fate.

name: Master Rune of Dragon Slaying
text: With this mighty rune the Dwarfs have slain many great wyrms and foul creatures of Chaos.

name: Master Rune of Flight
text: This cunning rune has been used to surprise the Dwarfs' foes from afar since the world was young.

name: Master Rune of Swiftness
text: This rune was first struck by Thurgrom the Hermit, the last runesmith to study Elven crafts.

name: Master Rune of Breaking
text: Runesmiths take pride in seeing the inferior works of other races break beneath a hammerblow.

name: Rune of Parrying
text: A weapon with this rune moves like quicksilver to block the attacks of the enemy.

name: Rune of Banishment
text: First crafted in the days of the Ancestor Gods, the touch of this potent rune is anathema to the creatures of Chaos.

name: Rune of Fury*
text: A Rune of Fury is an emblem of the anger all Dwarfs feel towards the enemies of their ancestors.

name: Grudge Rune*
text: Grudge Runes seek out the enemies of the Dwarfs with unerring accuracy.

name: Rune of Might*
text: A weapon inscribed with Runes of Might can fell a Giant in a flurry of blows.

name: Rune of Cleaving*
text: This rune was first made for mining tools, to allow them to break through rock with ease.

name: Rune of Striking*
text: A weapon with this rune moves to strike the foe's most vulnerable area with an uncanny precision.

name: Rune of Fire
text: Struck whilst the weapon glows white-hot, a Rune of Fire forever binds the heat of the forge.

name: Rune of Speed*
text: This rune enhances its bearer's awareness and quickness, catching unwary enemies by surprise.

name: Spear of Twilight
text: When this spear strikes, the fury and rage of the wielder is channelled into the body of the foe with appalling and destructive force.

name: Vaul's Wrath
text: Aggrieved by his fate, Vaul poured much of his rage into this mighty warbow during its creation.

name: Daith's Reaper
text: Amongst the most finely crafted weapons in all Athel Loren, this blade is said to be able to slice through steel without pause.

name: Blades of Loec
text: The swirling and delicate patterns inscribed into these matched swords assume a life of their own when sunlight falls upon them, dazzling and distracting the bearer's opponent.

name: Hunt Master's Pride
text: Gifted to those favoured by Orion, this enchanted spear has struck the dark hearts of many beasts over the centuries.

name: Bow of Loren
text: Strung with a single hair from the head of Ariel, Queen of Loren, the wielder of the Bow of Loren is the Fey Queen's champion, striking down her enemies with unnaturally swift precision.

name: Asyendi's Bane
text: A malicious spirit dwells within the body of this light and elegant bow, ever eager to punish its wielder if a shot misses its target.

name: Blades of Endless Flame
text: All in Athel Loren know how swiftly fire can consume all in its path when left unchecked, which is why the Wood Elves only turn to it under the most dire circumstance.

name: Helm of the Hunt
text: This enchanted great helm imbues its wearer with the furious spirit of Orion himself.

name: Mantle of Rebirth
text: This enchanted armour fills the wearer with the vitality of spring, instilling them with power and quickly healing their wound.

name: Railarian's Mantle
text: Woven by the eldest Branchwraiths, this armour is only gifted to one chosen by Athel Loren itself.

name: Cloak of Tumbling Leaves
text: The wearer draws upon the power of the wind to carry them across the battlefield with great haste.

name: Merciw's Locus
text: The eccentric Merciw held the concepts of personal honour and fairness in unusually high esteem.

name: Ariel's Favour
text: A token of the Mage Queen's goodwill, those who bear her gifts are shielded from the baleful magic of their enemies!

name: Glamourweave
text: Enemies that view this brooch become entranced by its hypnotic colours and patterns.

name: Orion's Favour
text: Those who bear the trinkets gifted by Orion know that their spear will strike true and their arrows will soar unhindered.

name: Tapestry of Talsyn
text: Known as the Groves of Eternity, Talsyn is the largest and most prosperous region of Athel Loren, its heraldry a rallying point for all the Asrai.

name: Banner of the Wildwood
text: The malevolence of the spirits that reside within the Wildwood clings to this tattered banner, chilling the souls of those that cast their gaze upon it.

name: Banner of the Wild Hunt
text: When the Horn of the Wild Hunt sounds, this great banner is unfurled and Orion's warriors feel the call of the hunt echo through their veins.

name: Banner of the Hunter King
text: The tattered threads of this ancient standard resound with Orion's eagerness for battle.

name: Standard of Morning's Chill
text: As the mists cloud the forest before dawn, so too do they now shield those faithful to Athel Loren from harm.

name: Banner of Springtide
text: Adorned with symbols of spring, this standard fills the Asrai with uncanny speed.

name: Banner of the Eternal Queen
text: This banner was woven by no lesser hand than that of Ariel, Mage Queen of Athel Loren.

name: Banner of Midsummer's Eve
text: Glimmering with the light of the midsummer sun, this shining pennant reveals the enemies of Athel Loren no matter where they may try to hide.

name: Crown of Antlers
text: A glittering circlet of gold adorned with a resplendent set of antlers, this exquisite headpiece is as deadly as it is beautiful.

name: Wraithstone
text: The screams of all who have met death within the bounds of Athel Loren emit from within this crystal.

name: Moonstone of the Hidden Ways
text: Some of the most mysterious hidden pathways within the depths of Athel Loren can only be unlocked with the power of ancient moonstones.

name: Blight-Tipped Arrows*
text: Coated in virulent toxins harvested from the depths of the Wild Wood, blight-tipped arrows need only nick the flesh to bring about a slow and painful death.

name: Hail of Doom Arrow*
text: When shot from a longbow, a Hail of Doom arrow splits into dozens of deadly shards.

name: Orb of Midsummer
text: At the height of summer, when the great forest is filled with life, the magic of the Wood Elves reaches the peak of its power.

name: Wailing Arrow*
text: These arrows wail when they draw blood, chilling the soul of even the bravest warrior.

name: Deepwood Sphere
text: This twisted and thorny orb of ebony wood speaks to the trees in the bitterest words of vengeance and spite, exhorting them to strike down all who intrude upon them.

name: Heartwood Pendant*
text: Crafted from the heartwood of an ancient oak felled deep within the darkest depths of the great forest, this pendant allows its bearer to tap into the power of Athel Loren itself.

name: Sigil of the Mage Queen
text: Upon the eve of battle, Ariel, beloved Queen of Athel Loren, infuses arcane vessels with a measure of her power. These tokens she gifts to her most favoured mages.

name: Oaken Stave
text: This crooked staff awakens the power of Athel Loren itself against those enemies that would use their magic to harm it.

name: Wand of Wych Elm
text: Fashioned from the innately magical Wych Elm tree, this humble wand allows its bearer to easily bend the Winds of Magic to their will.

name: Spear of Talsyn
text: Armour Bane (1), Magical Attacks

name: Storm's Wrath
text: Extra Attacks (+D3), Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands Notes: Storm's Wrath's Strength modifier applies only against enemy models the wielder charged this turn.

name: Judgement
text: Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2), Requires Two Hands

name: The Griffon Helm
text: Crafted for him after a crushing victory against the Orcs of the Bloody Tusk tribe on the borders of Reikland, the Griffon Helm has shielded Hans from many a mortal blow.

name: Mansmasher
text: Notes: This weapon's Strength modifier applies only during a turn in which its wielder charged.

name: The Skull of the Unicorn Lord
text: Atop his scarred scalp, Ghorros wears the broken remnants of the skull of Arsil, the prince of Unicorns.

name: The Dolorous Blade
text: The Dolorous Blade is a sword of great weight that glows with an eerie light. In battle, the Green Knight wields it with awesome strength and skill.

name: Beast Reaver
text: Armour Bane (1), Killing Blow, Magical Attacks Notes: When making attacks against Beastmen Brayherds, Harald Gemunsen may re-roll any failed rolls To Wound.

name: Mathlann's Ire
text: Armour Bane (1), Magical Attacks Notes: During the Combat phase, enemy models must re-roll a single successful roll To Hit made against the wielder of Mathlann's Ire.

name: Da Boss' Trophy Rack
text: Grisly trinkets swing from Kiknik's trophy rack, striking fear into his enemies' hearts.

name: Da Skull Smasha
text: Hammer Armour Bane (2), Magical Attacks Pick Armour Bane (2), Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (2) Notes: Da Skull Smasha has two profiles. You must choose which Kiknik will use when his combat is chosen during Step 1.1 of the Choose & Fight Combat sub-phase.

name: Chayal
text: Killing Blow, Requires Two Hands Notes: The wielder of Chayal may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1 made during the Combat phase.

name: The Pelt of Charandis
text: In recognition of Korhil's bravery and service, the pelt of Charandis was fashioned into a magnificent, enchanted cloak for him to wear.

name: Chalice of Brionne
text: This gilded relic draws power away from those who would harm the servants of the Lady.

name: The Staff of the Elements
text: This gnarled wooden staff acts as a conduit for the Winds of Magic, allowing Lady Élisse to better manifest the power of the elements.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Talons of the Storm
text: Armour Bane (1), Magical Attacks, Strike First

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: The Flail of Conquered Kings
text: The skulls of many conquered monarchs have been collected and cast in gold to create this macabre weapon. In Nekaph's skeletal hands, it continues to aid the Emissary in his grim duty.

name: Bog-wood Staff
text: Magical Attacks, Requires Two Hands Notes: For each unsaved Wound inflicted with this weapon, Ogdruz may recover a lost Wound.

name: Trollhide Shawl
text: Though it stinks, this bedraggled garment of rotted Trollhide helps keep Ogdruz safe from harm.

name: Cloak of Isha
text: Woven each spring by Ariel herself to herald the rebirth of her beloved husband, this sacred garment is the only protection Orion wears into battle.

name: Hawk's Talon
text: Magical Attacks, Multiple Shots (D3+1)

name: Horn of the Wild Hunt
text: When Orion sounds this great horn, all who hear it are imbued with a portion of Kurnous wildness and savagery.

name: The Spear of Kurnous
text: Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (D3) Magical Attacks, Multiple Wounds (D3) Notes: The Spear of Kurnous has two profiles, representing how this weapon is used in combat. The Spear of Kurnous shoots like a bolt thrower, using the Through & Through special rule.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: The Blessed Blade of Ptra
text: Blessed by the sun god, the blade of Ptra's white-hot edge is infused with the heat of the desert sun. It burns so brightly with divine fire that it blinds those who dare stand before the mighty Settra.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: The Chariot of the Gods
text: This mighty chariot is blessed by all of the gods and goddesses of Nehekhara, causing its wheels to blaze with divine flame.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: The Crown of Nehekhara
text: Having incorporated the crowns of conquered rivals into his own, Settra's regal headdress allows him to instil his undying will into all those nearby.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Tomb Kings of Khemri
name: The Scarab Brooch of Usirian
text: Made in the likeness of a skull-carapaced Khepra beetle, this talisman cloaks the wearer in protective energies and the blessing of Usirian, god of the Underworld.

name: Sorrow's End
text: This enchanted axe has brought about the end of all manner of monsters, both man and beast alike.

name: Dragonhide Cloak
text: After slaying the dragon Norndrak the Tyrant, Sir Cecil had the creature's hide fashioned into a cloak, which is said to be near-impervious to mundane weapons and can even ward off intense flame.

name: Grudge-Settler
text: This rune-encrusted weapon has long been wielded by the Kharls of the Dammaz Kron.

name: The Armour of Skaldour
text: Etched deeply with powerful runes, the Armour of Skaldour has turned countless blades and protected Thorgrim through many bloody battles.

name: The Axe of Dargo
text: The Axe of Dargo, a runic weapon of monstrous size, was made of the shards of King Baragor's broken axe, tempered with Dragon's blood and iron-oaths of vengeance.

name: The Slayer Crown
text: This majestic helmet has been worn by every Slayer King since the time of King Baragor.
